title: Cotgrave Place W.5365 Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including Plt Off. Robert Atkinson
narrative: In February 1941, B Flight of 12 (Bomber) Squadron was dispersing from RAF Binbrook in Lincolnshire to nearby RAF Tollerton, south of Nottingham. The Wellington was on a local familiarisation flight to acquaint the crew with the new area. During the flight, the aircraft suffered two separate control incidents, nearly diving into the ground on each occasion. The pilot, Squadron Leader Lawrence, managed to recover each time but was unable to avoid striking an oak tree while attempting to land back at Tollerton. The Wellington crashed near Cotgrave Place, the local manor house. Local farm workers rushed to the scene and attempted to rescue the crew, but seven (or eight) men were killed. Two crew members survived. This was one of the earliest wartime crashes in the Nottinghamshire area, occurring before the major expansion of Bomber Command training in the region.
cause_of_loss: Control problems during flight; struck oak tree attempting to return to Tollerton
operation_type: Local familiarisation flight for squadron dispersal
memorial_details: A memorial plaque is mounted on a plinth at the side of the first tee at Nottinghamshire Golf and Country Club (formerly Cotgrave Place). The plaque features the 12 Squadron fox-head badge and names all fatalities. The memorial is in good condition and accessible to golf club visitors.

external_narratives.summary: Memorial record for the crew of 12 (B) Squadron Wellington W5365 at Cotgrave Place. The memorial plaque is mounted on a plinth at the first tee of Nottinghamshire Golf and Country Club, featuring the 12 Squadron fox-head badge.
key_details: Plaque at first tee of golf club (formerly Cotgrave Place)

external_narratives.summary: International accident archive entry for Wellington II W5365 crash at Cotgrave on 8 February 1941. Records seven killed in the crash near Cotgrave Place manor house after the aircraft suffered repeated control problems and struck an oak tree.
key_details: Struck oak tree near Cotgrave Place

external_narratives.summary: Nottinghamshire aviation memorials guide including the Wellington W5365 memorial at Cotgrave. Part of a broader guide to aviation heritage sites across the county.
key_details: B Flight of 12 Squadron dispersed from Binbrook to Tollerton
